relationships.label: Hides Mara's bloodline
relationships.notes: Orin begins as a keeper of dangerous truth, then has to become a protector of Mara's right to choose what truth does next.

timelineVersions.label: Confesses and protects
timelineVersions.notes: Orin admits he erased Mara's record and shifts from gatekeeper to guard.

timelineVersions.label: Keeps the secret until Mara chooses
timelineVersions.notes: Orin stops managing the truth and guards Mara while she decides how much to reveal.

relationships.label: Frames the Crown as lost
relationships.notes: Lysandra keeps the Crown moving through civic storage sites while blaming rebels for the blue fire.

relationships.label: Forces Mara into the conspiracy
relationships.notes: The fire destroys Mara's neutral life and makes every ordinary delivery route dangerous.

relationships.label: Ignites the false riot
relationships.notes: The Crown rejects a command and throws blue fire through the west market mirrors.

relationships.label: Home route
relationships.notes: Mara knows the Glass Quarter by reflections, smells, shortcuts, and which bridges sing in rain.

relationships.label: Exiled from his own archive
relationships.notes: Orin knows the archive well enough to break in, but not well enough to leave unchanged.

relationships.label: Runs the salvage routes
relationships.notes: Sable's crews can move people, contraband, and rumor through the docks before the next bell.

relationships.label: Stole guild plans
relationships.notes: Tovan thinks he copied a lamp schematic. He actually copied part of a witness engine.

relationships.label: Sable owes Mara a clean escape
relationships.notes: Their bond begins as a practical debt and becomes the place both characters stop pretending risk is only tactical.

timelineVersions.label: Trusts with a knife drawn
timelineVersions.notes: Sable spends her escape money on a diversion and tells Mara the debt is not paid yet.

timelineVersions.label: Chooses each other openly
timelineVersions.notes: Sable gives Mara her true name in front of the bridges.

relationships.label: Minister of civic quiet
relationships.notes: Lysandra's authority is procedural until the Vault exposes how much procedure she has hollowed out.

timelineVersions.label: Betrays the Accord
timelineVersions.notes: Lysandra locks out the reform ministers and orders the militia to fire on witnesses.

relationships.label: Organizes against emergency rule
relationships.notes: The Choir's opposition is real, but the finale should make them choose charter work over permanent revolt.

relationships.label: Hides duplicate ledgers
relationships.notes: The Mirror Guild keeps backups of histories nobody is supposed to prove.

relationships.label: Decodes the ledger margin
relationships.notes: The shard reveals a fifth reflection beside every crossed-out heir.

relationships.label: Maps vows to gears
relationships.notes: The Ledger does not tell them where the Vault is. It tells them which promises make the route visible.

relationships.label: Unlocks the confession gear
relationships.notes: The key opens one air bell and one moral lock, then breaks.

relationships.label: Built for the Underclock
relationships.notes: The Crown is less an object than an interface for a city-scale witness engine.

relationships.label: Points to the eclipse shipment
relationships.notes: The Ledger names a bell shipment arriving while the moon covers the signal mirrors.

relationships.label: Reveals the Underclock route
relationships.notes: The bell shipment and the Lantern line up to show a blue path beneath the canals.

relationships.label: Makes dawn unavoidable
relationships.notes: Once the Vault projects the hidden record, the city has to answer in public.

relationships.label: Tentative field ally
relationships.notes: Mara accepts protection and pressure from the Choir without promising them a monarchy.

relationships.label: Wakes the Crown
relationships.notes: Mara's ownership is conditional: the Crown responds because she asks instead of commands.

relationships.label: Built from stolen plans
relationships.notes: Tovan's bright little crime becomes the key to the entire city engine.

relationships.label: Projects the fifth reflection
relationships.notes: Once the shard is set into the Lantern, the forgotten witness can finally be seen by more than Mara.

relationships.label: Names Mara as arsonist
relationships.notes: Lysandra makes Mara public enemy and accidental symbol at the same time.

timelineVersions.label: Publicly challenged
timelineVersions.notes: Lysandra loses control of the crowd but still chooses prosecution over surrender.

relationships.label: Polices the mirror bridges
relationships.notes: Emergency patrols turn the Glass Quarter from home into a watched stage.

relationships.label: Tracks midpoint leverage
relationships.notes: This planning note marks which alliances should become active or unstable by the midpoint.

relationships.label: Tests Mara's ending choice
relationships.notes: The theme note is linked to Mara so revisions keep the protagonist's choice connected to the memory motif.

relationships.label: Governs Archive bargains
relationships.notes: The rule defines why Bellwater memory contracts cannot be casually undone.

relationships.label: Governs memory records
relationships.notes: The Ledger proves traces and ownership, not full restoration.

relationships.label: Sets the bargain cost
relationships.notes: The event should preserve the rule's cost even when it reveals an echo.

relationships.label: Known by record keepers
relationships.notes: The Mirror Guild understands the difference between restored memory and reflected evidence.

relationships.label: Known by Lysandra
relationships.notes: Lysandra exploits the rule because victims cannot simply recover what she arranged to trade away.

relationships.label: Governs lantern navigation
relationships.notes: The Lantern matters because blue glass holds reflection lines through weather.

relationships.label: Governs storm routes
relationships.notes: The docks become dangerous when mirror lines fail or storms outrun bell cadence.

relationships.label: Known by the Choir
relationships.notes: The Choir relies on this rule for public trust and political leverage.
